name: Top Traffic Sources
description: Analyzes traffic sources to identify which channels drive the most visitors, sessions, and engagement. Essential for understanding marketing ROI and where to focus acquisition efforts. Breaks down by source/medium (Google/organic, Facebook/cpc, etc.).
sql_template:
WITH source_performance AS (
  SELECT 
    source,
    medium,
    campaign,
    COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) AS total_sessions,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users,
    SUM(pageviews) AS total_pageviews,
    AVG(pageviews) AS avg_pageviews_per_session,
    AVG(session_duration) AS avg_session_duration,
    SUM(CASE WHEN pageviews = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS bounced_sessions
  FROM sessions
  WHERE session_start >= '{{start_date}}'
    AND session_start < '{{end_date}}'
  GROUP BY source, medium, campaign
)
SELECT 
  source,
  medium,
  campaign,
  total_sessions,
  unique_users,
  total_pageviews,
  ROUND(avg_pageviews_per_session, 2) AS avg_pageviews,
  ROUND(avg_session_duration, 2) AS avg_duration_sec,
  ROUND(100.0 * bounced_sessions / NULLIF(total_sessions, 0), 2) AS bounce_rate_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * total_sessions / SUM(total_sessions) OVER (), 2) AS session_share_pct
FROM source_performance
ORDER BY total_sessions DESC
LIMIT {{limit}}

parameters.name: limit
parameters.label: Number of Sources
parameters.description: How many traffic sources to show

author: Sery Team
example_output:
Returns a table with columns:
- `source`: Traffic source (google, facebook, twitter, direct, etc.)
- `medium`: Medium (organic, cpc, referral, etc.)
- `campaign`: Campaign name (if applicable)
- `total_sessions`: Number of sessions from this source
- `unique_users`: Unique users from this source
- `total_pageviews`: Total pages viewed
- `avg_pageviews`: Average pageviews per session
- `avg_duration_sec`: Average session duration in seconds
- `bounce_rate_pct`: Percentage of single-page sessions (0-100)
- `session_share_pct`: Percentage of total traffic (0-100)
